idea-plugin.name: RepoBuddy
idea-plugin.description:
RepoBuddy — Run & Inspect Spring Data Repositories from IntelliJ

RepoBuddy lets you execute any Spring Data JPA repository method directly from the editor — without writing a test, opening a REST client, or redeploying your app. Click the gutter icon next to a repository method, fill in the parameters, and see the real query result, the captured SQL, and the execution time instantly.

Who is it for?

  • Spring Boot developers who debug data-layer issues daily
  • Teams building complex JPA queries who need fast feedback
  • Developers reviewing or refactoring repository logic
idea-plugin.plugin-features:
Plugin Features

  • Run ⌕ | ✎ gutter icon — every Spring Data repository method gets a clickable run marker. One click opens the execution popup.
  • Live execution against your running app — RepoBuddy injects a lightweight agent into your Spring Boot process. The real JPA context executes the method; no simulation or mocking.
  • SQL capture — Hibernate's StatementInspector intercepts every SQL statement triggered by the call. You see the exact queries, in order, with timestamps.
  • JSON result viewer — the return value is serialized and displayed with execution time colour-coded green / amber / red by speed.
  • Smart parameter form — input fields are generated per parameter type. Supports String, Long, Boolean, Pageable, UUID, LocalDate, enums, and arbitrary JSON objects.
  • Random data generation — fill fields instantly with type-aware sample values. Recognises 30+ name hints (email, phone, city, status, …) and known types (Pageable, UUID, LocalDate, BigDecimal, …). A Generate All button fills every field at once; individual per-field dice buttons let you randomise one parameter at a time.
  • Agent status indicator — the popup header shows a live ● Agent Ready / ● Offline badge so you know at a glance whether the agent is reachable before hitting Run.
  • Call chain tracer — pick any Spring MVC endpoint from the combo box and trace every repository method reachable from it. Results appear as an expandable tree with double-click navigation to source. HTTP method badges are colour-coded (GET=green, POST=blue, PUT=amber, DELETE=red). Expand All / Collapse All and Clear Cache controls are built in. A summary bar counts READ / WRITE / @Transactional calls and unique entity types.
  • Repository usage table — lists all repository methods with call-count badges (red = never called, amber = rarely called, green = frequently called). Live search filters by repository or method name. A Show Unused Only toggle lets you focus on dead code. Export to CSV with one click.
  • Zero configuration — the plugin automatically injects -javaagent into your run configurations on project open. No extra dependencies or properties needed. Compatible with IntelliJ 2024.1+ via the ProjectActivity API.
  • Colorful, theme-aware UI — all panels use a unified UITheme palette built on JBColor, so the plugin looks great in both IntelliJ Light and Dark themes with zero configuration.
idea-plugin.getting-started:
Getting Started

  1. Open a Spring Boot project. RepoBuddy patches your run configuration automatically.
  2. Start your app with the ▶ button. The agent binds to a free port and writes it to a temp file.
  3. Click the ⌕ | ✎ gutter icon next to any repository method.
  4. Fill in parameters (or hit 🎲 Generate All), then click Run.
  5. See the JSON result, captured SQL, and execution time — all in the popup.
idea-plugin.technical-information:
Technical Information

Supported parameter types:

String, Long, Integer, Boolean, Double, Float, Short, Byte, BigDecimal, UUID, LocalDate, LocalDateTime, ZonedDateTime, Pageable / PageRequest (with sort), Sort, Enum (resolved against real constants via reflection), List<String>, List<Long>, and any other type accepted as JSON.

Requirements:

  • IntelliJ IDEA 2024.1 or later (Community or Ultimate)
  • Java 17 or later
  • Spring Boot 3.x with Spring Data JPA on the classpath
  • App must be running locally (started from IntelliJ)
idea-plugin.contacts-and-resources:
Contacts & Resources

  • Issues & feature requests: GitHub Issues
  • Source code: github.com/ELglaly/RepoBuddy
  • Plugin page: JetBrains Marketplace
idea-plugin.change-notes:
1.0.0 — Initial Release

  • Run ⌕ | ✎ gutter icon on all Spring Data repository methods
  • Live execution via embedded Java agent (no Spring Security interference)
  • SQL capture via Hibernate StatementInspector
  • Smart parameter form with per-type widgets
  • Random data generation with 30+ name-hint rules (email, phone, city, status, UUID, …)
  • Per-field dice buttons + Generate All — fill one or all parameters in one click
  • Pageable / PageRequest support — JSON input deserialized into a real PageRequest with sort parsing; fixes Jackson InvalidDefinitionException on abstract interface
  • Enum resolution against real constants via reflection; falls back to first constant with a log listing all available values — never crashes on an unknown placeholder
  • Call chain tracer — expandable tree with colour-coded HTTP method badges (GET/POST/PUT/DELETE), Expand All / Collapse All, Clear Cache, and a summary bar (READ/WRITE/@Transactional/entities)
  • Repository usage table — live search filter, Show Unused Only toggle, Export CSV
  • Automatic run-configuration patching on project open (ProjectActivity, IntelliJ 2024.1+)
  • ● Agent Ready / Offline live status indicator in popup header
  • Execution time colour-coded green / amber / red (<100 ms / 100-499 ms / ≥500 ms)
  • Colorful, theme-aware UI via unified UITheme palette (JBColor — light & dark themes)

action.text: Trace Repository Calls for This API
action.description: Trace all repository methods reachable from this Spring endpoint
